name: What enumerate() yields
question_text: Given for i, v in enumerate(["a", "b"]):, what does each iteration bind?
general_feedback: enumerate(iterable, start=0) yields pairs (index, value). Here iteration yields (0, 'a') then (1, 'b'). The optional start argument changes the starting index.

answers.text: i is the index, v is the element
i and v are both elements
answers.feedback: enumerate produces an index alongside the element, not two elements.

answers.text: i is the element, v is the index
answers.feedback: Reversed — the unpacking order is (index, value).

answers.text: i is always 1 and v cycles
answers.feedback: i counts up from the start index (default 0), incrementing each iteration.
